Details: Removal of radium from water by precipitative softening can be very effective. In all referenced articles, filtration was used to some degree in the process, although the filter specifics were not always mentioned. Coupled with filtration, precipitative softening is able to achieve notable removal (75 to 96 percent). Lime softening is a Best Available Technology for the control of radium. Lime and lime/soda ash addition appeared to produce similar results. Radium removal is dependent on the initial concentration, lime and soda ash doses, and the presence of co-occuring solutes, such as manganese and iron.
